commit.message:
fix(seal): respect [[seal.override]] fields from config when sealing

cmd_seal was only reading --fields from the CLI flag, ignoring any
[[seal.override]] entries in .gitvault/config.toml. This caused
`gitvault seal <file>` to encrypt all string values even when the
config restricted sealing to specific fields.

Fix: after computing the repo-relative path, look up matching
[[seal.override]] entries from config and use them as the field list
when no --fields CLI flag is provided. CLI --fields takes precedence.

Same fix applied to cmd_unseal for consistency.

Added regression test: test_cmd_seal_respects_config_override_fields

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: gitvault
repository.description: Git-native secrets manager for teams and AI agents: age encryption, deterministic diffs, leak prevention, secure runtime injection. Dual-licensed MIT OR Apache-2.0.

commit.message:
feat: implement P0 critical interpreter opcodes (closes #282) (#321)

Implement 13 high-priority opcodes that were hitting the catch-all
'unimplemented opcode' arm in the interpreter dispatch loop:

- DeletePropertySloppy / DeletePropertyStrict: delete named properties
- CreateRestParameter: collect surplus args into a rest array
- CreateMappedArguments / CreateUnmappedArguments: build arguments objects
- ThrowReferenceErrorIfHole: TDZ check for let/const
- ThrowSuperNotCalledIfHole / ThrowSuperAlreadyCalledIfNotHole: super() guards
- CallProperty0 / CallProperty1 / CallProperty2: fixed-arity property calls
- CallRuntime: stub for internal runtime dispatch
- StaNamedOwnProperty: own property definition
- StaLookupSlot: scope-chain variable store

Also fixes a pre-existing unused-import clippy warning by gating
MaglevJitCodeCache and TurbofanJitCodeCache imports behind cfg.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ProphecyEngine): use IsCompletedSuccessfully for timeout-safe Result access

Phase 2 timeout catch allows continuing with partial results, but .Result
calls would block on incomplete tasks. Use IsCompletedSuccessfully guard
to safely return null for unfinished tasks (handled by existing fallbacks).

Add comprehensive telemetry session tests (#177)

* Add comprehensive telemetry session tests

Add 53 new tests covering telemetry session recording, replay,
and analysis in the telemetry-recorder crate:

- Session lifecycle: start/stop, restart, frames-before-start
- Recording format: JSON save/load roundtrip, flags preservation
- Replay fidelity: frame order, data match, progress tracking, reset
- Session metadata: game ID, car/track, duration, timestamps
- Multiple simultaneous sessions: independent recorders and players
- Large session handling: 5K-10K frame sessions with roundtrip
- Export formats: CSV, compact JSON, and binary (length-prefixed)
- Session comparison/diff: identical, metadata, frame data, symmetry
- Cross-format roundtrips: JSON↔binary equivalence
- Fixture scenarios: all TestScenario variants verified

New library features added to TelemetryRecording:
- to_csv(): Export as CSV string
- to_compact_json(): Export as compact JSON bytes
- to_binary()/from_binary(): Length-prefixed binary format
- diff(): Compare two recordings returning SessionDiff

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: replace deprecated .sequence field access with frame_index

The regression-prevention CI check flags .sequence as a deprecated
TelemetryData field. Replace f.sequence in CSV export with an
enumerate index (frame_index) and update tests accordingly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Parallelize Phase 2 startup tasks and remove arbitrary delays

- Remove Task.Delay() calls from CacheWarming (500ms), MachineLearning (2000ms), and DynamicConfig (1500ms)
- Replace sequential foreach with Task.WhenAll() so all 4 init tasks run in parallel
- Preserve per-task try-catch for independent failure handling

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add exception safety to fire-and-forget event invocations in PaperTradingService

Wrap 5 event invocations (TradeClosed x2, TradeOpened x1, PriceUpdated x2)
in try-catch blocks to prevent subscriber exceptions from crashing the
service or breaking trade flow. Exceptions are logged as warnings via _logger.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Parallelize asset data loading in SmartTrainingService

Replace sequential foreach loop with bounded parallel loading using
SemaphoreSlim(4) and Task.WhenAll. Use ConcurrentBag for thread-safe
result collection. Independent I/O operations now run concurrently
with max 4 concurrent loads.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 30s timeout to ProphecyEngine Phase 2 Task.WhenAll

Wrap the Phase 2 parallel analysis Task.WhenAll with a linked
CancellationTokenSource and WaitAsync to prevent indefinite hangs
if a service deadlocks. On timeout, logs a warning and adds a
partial-results notice to prophecy.Warnings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use /api/group/pause whenever group_id is set (#50)

* fix: use /api/group/pause whenever group_id is set, not just groupSize > 1

Previously onDevicePause() fell back to /api/pause when the locally
visible group had only 1 member (e.g. after MA restructured groups
post-pause). If group_id is non-null the player is in an MA group and
/api/group/pause must always be used — MA owns group membership.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(engine): add comprehensive device auto-detection tests (#176)

* test(engine): add comprehensive device auto-detection tests

Add 46 tests covering device auto-detection, enumeration, and matching
logic across all 15+ supported vendors:

- VID/PID matching for Fanatec, Moza, Simagic, Logitech, Thrustmaster,
  VRS, Simucube, Cammus, Asetek, Heusinkveld, OpenFFBoard, FFBeast,
  PXN, Leo Bodnar, Button Box, and AccuForce
- Device type classification (wheelbase, pedal, shifter, handbrake)
- Multi-device scenarios (same vendor and mixed vendor)
- Hot-plug enumeration (add, remove, add-remove-add cycle, sequential)
- Priority ordering for shared VIDs (0x0483, 0x16D0, 0x1209)
- Fallback behavior (unknown VID, HID PID fallback, unknown PID)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: illustration character appearance — flatten avatar attributes for backend

Flutter mapper (wizard_data_mapper.dart):
- GeneratedAvatar.toJson() nests hair/skin under 'attributes' with
  snake_case keys; backend reads camelCase at avatar root level
- Build flatAvatar from avatarJson + promoted attribute keys:
  hair_color→hairColor, hair_style→hairStyle, skin_tone→skinColor,
  outfit→topType; send this as characterDetails['avatar']

Backend (gemini_image_generator.py):
- Extract avatar reading into _read_avatar_details() helper
- Helper checks both camelCase root keys AND nested 'attributes' dict
  (handles both old and new payload formats gracefully)
- Replace two duplicate blocks with helper calls

Result: hairstyle, hair color, skin tone, and outfit from a generated
avatar are now correctly embedded in every illustration prompt.

commit.message:
test: comprehensive live file reloading test coverage

22 new tests exercising the complete live reload pipeline:

disk_io.rs (5 tests):
  - Large file (1MB+) stable read correctness
  - Empty file read handling
  - Binary/invalid UTF-8 file rejection
  - Deleted file error path
  - Large file atomic write round-trip

live_merge.rs (4 tests):
  - 25K+ line merge fallback to whole-file conflict
  - Disk truncated to empty during dirty buffer
  - 1000-line identical edits merge cleanly
  - Multiple disjoint edits at different positions

main.rs (8 tests):
  - Clean reload invalidates all caches + advances edit_seq
  - Dirty buffer disjoint disk change merges cleanly
  - Overlapping edits trigger conflict dialog
  - Empty disk file while dirty preserves user edits
  - Successive reloads maintain edit_seq monotonicity
  - Large file (50K lines) reload maintains stats integrity

render/mod.rs (5 tests):
  - Cache reparse on content change at mid-scroll position
  - Scroll-past-end after content shrinks (no panic)
  - Content growth at 5 different scroll fractions
  - 20 rapid content switches (simulates rapid file saves)
  - Empty content reload clears blocks and height

fix: remove dying /bin/true procd instance that breaks UCI reload triggers (#30)

* fix: remove dummy /bin/true procd instance that causes service to die and breaks UCI triggers

The start_service() in /etc/init.d/tailscale-settings created a procd instance
running /bin/true with respawn 3600 1 3. Since /bin/true exits in milliseconds
(way below the 3600s threshold), every exit counted as a crash. After 3 crashes,
procd marks the service as dead and may stop processing its UCI config-change
triggers (registered via procd_add_reload_trigger), meaning LuCI Save & Apply
never called reload_service() → apply_settings() → tailscale set.

Fix: remove the procd instance. tailscale-settings is a config-only service.
The trigger registration happens in _procd_close_service() via service_triggers()
regardless of whether any procd instances exist.

Co-authored-by: Tokisaki-Galaxy <61621971+Tokisaki-Galaxy@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: labeled break/continue statements (closes #269) (#318)

Implement labeled break and continue in the bytecode generator. The parser
already handles the syntax; this adds the label_map and pending_label_name
mechanism so that break/continue with labels resolve to the correct bytecode
jump targets.

Changes:
- Add label_map HashMap and pending_label_name to FunctionCompiler
- Add compile_labeled() for Stmt::Labeled with loop detection
- Add patch_pending_label() called by each loop compiler to register the
  continue-label for labeled continues
- Update compile_break/compile_continue to resolve labeled targets
- Add 6 tests covering labeled break, labeled continue, nested labels,
  continue-on-non-loop error, and duplicate-label error

commit.message:
Security: Fix command injection via LLM output in summary workflow (#167)

* Initial plan

* 🔒 security(workflow): fix command injection in summary.yml

Replace direct LLM output interpolation in shell command with the
already-defined RESPONSE env variable. Previously,
`${{ steps.inference.outputs.response }}` was single-quote-delimited
inside a `run` block, allowing a crafted LLM response containing a
single quote to escape the quoting context and inject arbitrary shell
commands (potentially leaking GITHUB_TOKEN).

The RESPONSE env var was already present in the `env` block but unused.
This change references it as `"$RESPONSE"` and also double-quotes
`"$ISSUE_NUMBER"` for consistency.

Fixes: command injection per security report.

Co-authored-by: SamErde <20478745+SamErde@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: SamErde <20478745+SamErde@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: decompose monolithic transformers into composed facade pattern (Phase 10)

Introduce reusable sub-transforms following the audio/text sibling repo pattern:
- ImagePreprocessingTransformer: MLImage -> preprocessed tensor
- OnnxImageScoringTransformer: tensor -> raw ONNX output (with batch support)

All 5 task transformers now compose these sub-transforms:
  Estimator.Fit() creates sub-estimators sequentially
  Transformer methods call preprocessor.Preprocess() -> scorer.Score() -> PostProcess()

Convenience APIs (Classify, Detect, Segment, etc.) preserved with same signatures.
All 144 tests pass with zero regressions.

Fix two compiler bugs that caused LVGL navigation code to fail:

1. Missing is/is not operators in method expression builder
   - _build_method_expr() op_map was missing ast.Is and ast.IsNot
   - Caused 'x is None' in methods to emit '==' instead of 'is'

2. Over-eager unboxing of mp_obj_t args in self method calls
   - _emit_self_method_call() defaulted to mp_int_t target type
   - Now uses arg.ir_type.to_c_type_str() for correct type handling

Add unit tests for both IR builder and emitter to prevent regressions.
Add blog post documenting the bugs and fixes.

Document the complete work done in this branch:
- Navigation system with push/pop/replace operations
- MVU (Model-View-Update) pattern implementation
- Tree navigation rules enforcement

commit.message:
feat: remove assistant-ui, build custom chat UI (#87)

## Major refactor: remove @assistant-ui/react dependency

Replace assistant-ui with custom React components for full VS Code
parity.

### What changed
- **Removed** @assistant-ui/react and @assistant-ui/react-markdown
- **New** src/components/chat/ with: MessageList, ChatComposer,
AssistantMessage, UserMessage, MarkdownContent, ToolGroup, ToolProgress,
ActionBar
- **New** ChatActionsContext for send/cancel/edit callbacks
- **Per-message thinking** — thinkingText on ChatMessage, not global
ThinkingContext
- **Send while running** — just works (no library blocking it)
- **Stop button** — immediate cancel
- **Simplified useOfficeChat** — no more useExternalStoreRuntime adapter

### Tests
- 752/752 integration ✅
- 40/42 Playwright ✅ (1 pre-existing flaky, 1 timing)

---------

Co-authored-by: Stefan Broenner <stefan.broenner@microsoft.comm>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
VPLAY-12854 getfile to use std vector follow-up (#1130)

VPLAY-12854 Change GetFile to use std::vector instead of AampGrowableBuffer

Reason for Change: Refactors AAMP’s download buffer plumbing to use std::vector<uint8_t> end-to-end (notably PrivateInstanceAAMP::GetFile and CurlCallbackContext), aligning core download paths, cache handling, and unit tests with the newer growable-buffer maturation work.

Test Guidance: this is pure tech debt - need do-no-harm general regression testing and confirmation that no impact on existing L1/L2/L3 tests

Risk: Medium (many changes - should be safe/compiler guided, but big change set nevertheless)

---------

Signed-off-by: Philip Stroffolino <philip_stroffolino@cable.comcast.com>
Co-authored-by: DomSyna <192202460+DomSyna@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
